title: S.Diwakar vs N.K.Latha on 18 October, 2019
body: S.Diwakar vs N.K.Latha on 18 October, 2019 Author: V.Bhavani Subbaroyan Bench: V.Bhavani Subbaroyan TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 IN THE HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE AT MADRAS DATED : 18.10.2019 CORAM THE HON'BLE MRS.JUSTICE V.BHAVANI SUBBAROYAN TR.C.M.P.SR.No.76052 of 2018 S.Diwakar ... Petitioner Vs. N.K.Latha ... Respondent Transfer Civil Miscellaneous Petition is filed under Section 23 of the Code of Civil Procedure, to call for the papers in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 on the file of the Principal Family Judge, Chennai and to set aside the order dated 03.09.2018 in Memo SR.No.15145 of 2018 in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018, order dated 05.09.2018 in I.A.SR.No.15297 of 2018 by the II Additional Family Court, Chennai and order dated 18.09.2018 in Tr.O.P.SR.No.51322 of 2018 by the Principal Sessions Cum District Judge, Chennai, by orders passed transferring the I.A.Nos.2836, 2837, 2838 of 2018 in dismissed I.A.Nos.812, 813 of 2014 in dismissed O.P.No.601 of 2007 on the file of II Additional Family Court, Chennai, to some other Court except the Principal Family Court and III Additional Court in the premises. For Petitioner : Mr.S.Diwakar Party-in-Person http://www.judis.nic.in 1/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 ORDER This Transfer Civil Miscellaneous Petition has been filed seeking for a prayer to call for the papers in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 on the file of the Principal Family Judge, Chennai and to set aside the order dated 03.09.2018 in Memo SR.No.15145 of 2018 in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018, order dated 05.09.2018 in I.A.SR.No.15297 of 2018 by the II Additional Family Court, Chennai and order dated 18.09.2018 in Tr.O.P.SR.No.51322 of 2018 by the Principal Sessions Cum District Judge, Chennai, by orders passed transferring the I.A.Nos.2836, 2837, 2838 of 2018 in dismissed I.A.Nos.812, 813 of 2014 in dismissed O.P.No.601 of 2007 on the file of II Additional Family Court, Chennai, to some other Court except the Principal Family Court and III Additional Court in the premises. 2. Heard the petitioner who is an advocate, Party-in-person, at length and he has furnished the written submissions before this Court. 3. The Registry of this Court has raised the question of maintainability of the Transfer Petition filed by the petitioner. As http://www.judis.nic.in 2/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 such, the petitioner is with the grievance that the respondent filed O.P.No.601 of 2007 before the III Additional Family Court, Chennai, seeking for dissolution of marriage between them on the ground of cruelty and the same was dismissed for default by order dated 06.12.2013. Later, the respondent seems to have filed an application in I.A.No.812 of 2014 to restore the Original O.P., along with an application in I.A.No.813 of 2014 to condone the delay in filing the petition for restoration. Pending I.As., the respondent seems to have filed Tr.O.P.No.55 of 2016 and the same was ordered to be transferred from the file of the III Additional Family Court to the file of the II Additional Family Court. However, the petitioner herein has filed a petition in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 before the Principal Family Court, Chennai, seeking transfer of the above said applications from the file of the II Additional Family Court, Chennai, to the file of the I Additional Family Court. 4. It is seen from the records that the said O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 was returned for certain compliances by the learned Principal Judge on 04.06.2018 and as against the order of return, the petitioner seems to have approached this Court by way of an appeal in CMA.SR.No.46928 of 2018 and this Court by order dated 19.07.2018 dismissed the same in the SR stage itself. Thereafter, it http://www.judis.nic.in 3/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 is seen that the petitioner has filed a Memo in Memo Sr.No.15145 of 2018 in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 before the Family Court, to take up the O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 on file and to number the same, but it was not recorded by the learned Principal Judge as the petitioner has not complied the orders dated 04.06.2018. Subsequently, the petitioner has filed an Interlocutory Application in I.A.No.15297 of 2018 before the II Additional Family Court, Chennai, to transfer the O.P.No.601 of 2007 and the I.A.Nos.2836, 2837 and 2838 of 2018 filed before the same Court from that Court to some other Court except the Principal Judge Court, and it was also came to be dismissed on 05.09.2018. 5. It is further seen that after the order of dismissal of the above application, the petitioner has filed Tr.O.P.SR.No.51322 of 2018 before the Principal Sessions Cum District Judge, Chennai, to transfer the above said applications from the file of the II Additional Family Court to some other Court except the Principal Family Court, and the same was rejected on 18.09.2018 as not maintainable. Challenging the same, the petitioner has filed this petition before this Court seeking to set aside the order dated 18.09.2018 made in Tr.O.P.SR.No.51322 of 2018 along with the earlier orders passed in http://www.judis.nic.in 4/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 Memo Sr.No.15145 of 2018 in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 dated 03.09.2018 and I.A.SR.No.15297 of 2018 dated 05.09.2018. 6. It is seen from the prayers sought for in this petition that the petitioner has come forward to challenge the orders passed in the applications which are all in the S.R. Stage. Further, the petitioner seems to have grievance with almost all the Presiding Officers wherever his case is listed. Hence, the prayer sought for in this petition to transfer the I.A.Nos.2836, 2837 and 2838 of 2018 in dismissed I.A.Nos.812 and 813 of 2014 in O.P.No.601 2007 is not maintainable before this Court. As far as the prayer sought to set aside the order passed in the Memo Sr.No.15145 of 2018 in O.P.SR.No.9202 of 2018 and in the other petitions in preliminary stages where rejection order has been passed by the Family Court, the petitioner invoking Section 23 of the Code of Civil Procedure, which is as hereunder : “ Section 23 of Code of Civil Procedure 1908 "To what Court application lies" (1) Where the several Courts having jurisdiction are subordinate to the same Appellate Court, an application under section 22 shall be made to the Appellate Court. http://www.judis.nic.in 5/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 (2) Where such Courts are subordinate to different Appellate Courts but to the same High Court, the application shall be made to the said High Court. (3) Where such Courts are subordinate to different High Courts, the application shall be made to the High Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the Court in which the suit is brought is situate.” 7. The prayer made by the petitioner is not maintainable for the reason that Section 23 of the Civil Procedure Code vests the power to the Appellate Court to be approached where the several Courts having Jurisdiction are Subordinate to the same Appellate Court where the suit may be instituted in anyone or two or more Courts and has instituted in one such Courts and any defendant after notice all the other party before or settlement of issues applied to have the suit transferred to other Court, as such application seeking transfer shall be made under Section 23 . 8. As far as the present case is concerned, the prayer setting aside the orders passed by the Family Court may be an order passed in the preliminary stage before the original petitions being http://www.judis.nic.in 6/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 numbered cannot be challenged under Section 23 of the Code of Civil Procedure. As such orders passed even in a memo or in the preliminary stage of original petitions being numbered from maintainability or any order which is not appealable cannot be challenged under Section 23 of the Code of Civil Procedure as done by the petitioner herein, and the petitioner has got appropriate remedy under the Civil Procedure Code itself. As such, the prayer under Section 23 of the Code of Civil Procedure is not maintainable. 9. Accordingly, this Transfer Civil Miscellaneous Petition SR.No.76052/18 is rejected and the Registry is directed to return the papers to the petitioner. So that he can take appropriate course of action if he desires to do so. 18.10.2019 raja Index : yes/no Internet : yes/no To 1. The Presiding Officer, Family Court, Chennai. 2. The Presiding Officer, II Additional Family Court, Chennai. 3. The Presiding Officer, Principal City Civil Cum Sessions Court, Chennai. http://www.judis.nic.in 7/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 V.BHAVANI SUBBAROYAN.J., raja TR.C.M.P.SR.No.76052 of 2018 http://www.judis.nic.in 8/9 TR.C.M.P.No.365 of 2019 18.10.2019 http://www.judis.nic.in 9/9
